hypothesis: If class-balancing strategies (such as template-frequency-weighted sampling or loss reweighting during training) are applied to retrosynthesis models trained on USPTO-retro, then top-k accuracy for rare reaction templates (frequency 1–10) will increase substantially without a corresponding decrease in accuracy for common templates, thereby reducing the performance disparity between rare and frequent template classes that currently spans up to 88.6% for template-based models and 55.4% for template-free models.
